title: Untar archive with symlink to mounted volume results into Permission denied error on MacOs
body:
### Issue Description

Hi,
If I try to untar an archive with symlinks included to an volume that is shared with the host MacOs I get a permission error.

This is similar to https://github.com/docker/for-mac/issues/6277

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. start a podman container with a mounted volume on MacOs (e.g. podman run -it -v /Volumes:/Volumes ubuntu /bin/bash)
2. `cd /Volumes`
3. ```
# Create the directory structure that triggers the bug.
mkdir demo
touch demo/src
mkdir demo/subdir
cd demo/subdir && ln -s ../src ./dst
cd ..
# Create the archive and remove the source files.
tar -czvf demo.tgz ./demo
rm -rf ./demo

# Attempt to unpack the archive.
tar -xvf demo.tgz
```


### Describe the results you received

```
./demo/
./demo/subdir/
./demo/subdir/dst
tar: ./demo/subdir/dst: Cannot open: Permission denied
./demo/src
tar: Exiting with failure status due to previous errors
```

### Describe the results you expected

There should be no error and the symlinks should be created. Untarring the archive to a folder that is not in the mounted volume works (e.g. cd ~ && tar -xvf demo.tgz)

comments.body:
In order to reproduce:
```
# Create the directory structure that triggers the bug.
mkdir demo
touch demo/src
mkdir demo/subdir
cd demo/subdir
ln -s ../src ./dst

# Create the archive and remove the source files.
tar -czvf demo.tgz ./demo
rm -rf ./demo

# Attempt to unpack the archive.
tar -xvf demo.tgz
```
there are limitations of virtiofsd; i am not sure if this is one of them or not, but my guess is that the permissions cannot be satisfied?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
